description: Stark Research Labs workstation 01 — staging machine used in APT compromise by CRIMSON OSPREY

iocs.description: Companion malware in fake perfmon directory
7-Zip in attacker staging directory (data archiving for exfil)
Malware installer masquerading as Windows DLL (advapi32)
Attacker staging directory for tools and malware
Fake performance monitor directory containing malware
Domain admin account used for lateral movement

iocs.value: McAfee agent files with zeroed timestamps

iocs.description: PowerShell execution log deleted — hides script-based attack evidence
Task Scheduler log deleted — hides persistence mechanism evidence
Zero reconstructable logon/account events despite years of multi-user activity
Files overwritten with zeros (not just unlinked) — active evidence destruction
SQL service account profile removed — anomalous cleanup suggesting attacker access
Classified corporate documents staged in private folder (wire transfers, board notes)
Reconnaissance tool downloaded by user mhill (2018-08-08)

notes: This workstation shows DUAL compromise indicators: (1) External APT (CRIMSON OSPREY) using staging dirs for malware + tools, with systematic anti-forensic log clearing. (2) Insider threat (mhill) staging corporate documents, downloading OSINT tools, and accessing classified materials. STUN.exe, msedge.exe masquerading, and pssdnsvc.exe are likely on other hosts (DC, RD servers) in the SRL-2018 case.
